stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.

stories.headline: China Mine Explosion Sees Coking Coal Jump by Daily Limit
stories.summary: China coking coal jumped by the daily limit after a deadly mine blast in Shanxi province sparked fears of broader supply disruptions from heightened safety inspections across the sector.

stories.headline: Brazil debt crisis swells with over 82 million behind on payments
AI fever and off-exchange boom grip Japanese retail traders
stories.summary: Average daily trading volume on Tokyo Stock Exchange’s prime market almost doubles in 12 months

stories.headline: Guzman y Gomez faces class action from US workers over closed stores
stories.summary: Mexican-themed Australian fast food chain accused of terminating staff without adequate pay or notice as it pulls out of the USAmerican workers at Guzman y Gomez’s shuttered US stores have launched a class action lawsuit against the Mexican-themed Australian fast food chain over allegations staff were terminated without adequate pay or notice.The legal claim, filed in a US federal trial court in Illinois, was sparked by GyG’s decision last week t

stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirmed on Sunday that Russia had used the powerful nuclear-capable Oreshnik medium-range missile in its overnight attack, as well as hundreds of drones and dozens of other missiles.

stories.headline: Russia hits Kyiv with hypersonic ballistic missile in ‘deranged’ attack
stories.summary: Assault hits water facility, market, residential buildings and schools, killing at least four and injuring dozens Russia used its powerful hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile for a third time in Ukraine as part of a massive attack on Kyiv and its surrounding region that killed at least four people and injured about 100.Russia hit the city of Bila Tserkva in the Kyiv region with the missile, Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, said. stories.headline: Trump says Iran deal 'largely negotiated' including reopening Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Tehran also signals progress on talks but says the key issue of nuclear weapons is not part of an initial framework it is working on.

stories.headline: How will Iran war fallout impact upcoming US elections?
stories.summary: Trump purges Republican critics, but are the prospects for his party in upcoming elections heading up or down?

stories.headline: Zelenskyy speaks to Al Jazeera at site of major Russian attacks in Kyiv
stories.summary: Russia has launched one of its largest attacks on Kyiv since the war began, firing hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.headline: Trump says US will not ‘rush into a deal’ with Iran as talks continue
stories.summary: Price of oil falls on hopes of an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: European Gas Declines on Optimism US and Iran Will Reach Deal
stories.summary: European natural gas dropped during early trading in Asia on optimism that the US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: LIVE: Trump says Iran deal not ‘fully negotiated yet’
stories.summary: US president says any potential agreement with Tehran will be 'good and proper' as mediation efforts continue.

stories.headline: Inside a Beirut barbershop shaped by war and crisis
stories.summary: For nearly 20 years, Mario Habib has run a barbershop through wars, economic collapse and political crisis in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Turkish police storm main opposition CHP’s party headquarters
stories.summary: Violence erupted after Turkish police stormed the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party in Ankara.

stories.headline: Gaza flotilla activists faced police violence upon return to Spain. Israel: “It’s hypocrisy"
stories.summary: A group of Spanish activists from the Gaza flotilla, expelled from Israel, clashed with police at Bilbao airport, resulting in beatings by officers and four arrests.

stories.headline: For third year in a row, Israel blocks Hajj pilgrimage for Gaza Muslims
stories.summary: As millions of Muslims gather in Mecca, Palestinians in Gaza face a third year of heartbreak as Israel has shut border.

stories.headline: Turkish riot police storm opposition offices after leaders ousted
stories.summary: Officers forced their way into the building days after the party vowed to defy a court ruling removing its leaders.

stories.headline: BBC at the site of China's worst mining disaster in more than a decade
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed after a gas explosion at the Liushenyu mine in China.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak poses massive challenges, warns nurse
stories.summary: Kate White says she is "extremely concerned about the inability to get resources" to the Democratic Republic of Congo.

stories.headline: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
stories.summary: The 39-year-old was fatally injured at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland, apparently while spearfishing.

stories.headline: California declares emergency as fire crews race to contain toxic leak
stories.summary: Thousands have been evacuated as officials warn a tank containing about 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate could explode.

stories.headline: Oil Drops as US Touts Progress on Deal Toward Reopening Strait
stories.summary: Oil dropped as the US and Iran edged toward a deal, although President Donald Trump said that Washington’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would remain until an agreement was completed.

stories.headline: Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows
stories.summary: More than 20,000 attacks on markets, farmland and food distribution systems have been recorded since 2018Hunger is being increasingly exploited as a weapon of war with more than 20,000 documented incidents of “food-related violence” in the past eight years, new analysis reveals.Attacks include 1,261 strikes on markets used by families for daily groceries and 863 incidents in which food distribution systems were targeted and workers killed. Contin

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon amid fresh evacuation orders
stories.summary: Israel's attacks in southern Lebanon are expanding despite supposed ceasefire.

stories.headline: Turkish police storm main opposition headquarters with tear gas
stories.summary: Officers used tear gas inside the CHP building in Ankara to enforce a controversial judicial decision reinstating former leader Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu. The intrusion has sparked fierce resistance from current party officials, who accuse Kılıçdaroğlu of deploying "mafia thugs" to seize power.

stories.headline: Pakistan train bombing kills at least 24 people
stories.summary: Pakistan's prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, said he strongly condemned the "heinous bomb explosion" and sent his condolences to the victims' families.

stories.headline: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
stories.summary: The Oreshnik missile, which reportedly travels over 10 times the speed of sound, was used, Russia has confirmed.

stories.headline: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
stories.summary: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

stories.headline: Xi railed against Japan’s ‘remilitarisation’ at Trump summit
stories.summary: Chinese president became heated when criticising US ally for its increased defence spending

stories.headline: Japan Regulator Urges Firms to Use Cash for Growth, Not Returns
stories.summary: Japan’s financial regulator is urging the country’s listed companies to spend more of their cash piles on long-term business investment instead of rewarding shareholders with buybacks and higher dividends.

stories.headline: Rubio Invites India’s Modi to Visit White House as Ties Improve
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi on Saturday and invited him to visit the White House, as the two sides work to improve ties. Bloomberg News State Department Reporter Eric Martin joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss Rubio's four-day visit to the world’s most-populous nation for talks with senior Indian officials on issues ranging from energy to regional

stories.headline: Gunman in Shooting Near White House Killed in Exchange of Fire With Secret Service
stories.summary: A gunman known to the U.S. Secret Service opened fire near the White House and was shot and killed by federal officers. President Trump was in the White House at the time.

stories.headline: Thousands rally in Madrid demanding Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez resign
stories.summary: Protesters march down the streets of Madrid calling for socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez to resign over allegations of corruption within his entourage.

stories.headline: Turkish police use tear gas to break into CHP headquarters
stories.summary: Turkish riot police used tear gas and forced their way into the Ankara headquarters of the main opposition CHP party after a court dismissed its leadership, as supporters blocked entrances in defiance of the ruling.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: Uber weighs higher bid for Delivery Hero after €11.5bn offer rebuffed
stories.summary: San Francisco-based group approached major shareholder in German food group

stories.headline: Stocks Rise, Oil Falls on Iran Deal Expectations: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks climbed and crude oil fell after US officials signaled that a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows may be near.

stories.headline: Japan Bond Yield Surge Deepens Regional Bank Stock Divide
stories.summary: Japan’s rising bond yields are likely to widen the stock performance gap between regional lenders with weaker investment portfolios and those with stronger holdings, according to analysts.

stories.headline: Indonesia to Update Markets on Commodity Export Control
stories.summary: Indonesia’s Trade Vice Minister Dyah Roro Esti Widya Putri says the government is finalizing a centralized export agency for palm oil, coal, and ferroalloys. Speaking to Bloomberg’s Stephen Engle at APEC in China, she stated the plan is moving to execution despite policy shifts that have unnerved investors. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indonesia to Lay Out Commodity Export Policy Soon, Official Says
stories.summary: Global markets and major trading partners can expect updates on Indonesia’s new commodity export policy within weeks as the government works on the legal and structural rollout of its new centralized export agency, according to a trade ministry official.

stories.headline: Paz Says Protests Test Democracy Ahead of Talks
stories.summary: Bolivia President Rodrigo Paz tells Wall Street Week why pragmatism — not ideology — will define his government, and why closing the chapter on 20 years of one-party rule won’t be easy. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Jardine Is Said to Near Purchase of I-MED in $2.4 Billion Deal
stories.summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings Ltd. is nearing an agreement to buy I-MED Radiology Network Ltd., people familiar with the deal said on Sunday, an acquisition that would add Australia’s biggest medical diagnostic imaging provider to the conglomerate’s portfolio.

stories.headline: Europe's biggest beach: endless sand on Germany's North Sea coast
stories.summary: A sandbank the size of 1,400 football pitches: Kniepsand on the North Frisian island of Amrum looks like a slice of desert and shields the island, while its vast beach could give tourism further inland a boost.

stories.headline: Rubio meets Modi during India visit with energy high on agenda
stories.summary: The secretary of state wants to sell US energy to Delhi to make up for shortfalls caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
stories.summary: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

stories.headline: ‘Every health facility said they were full’: alarm over rapid spread of Ebola in DRC
stories.summary: New strain of virus, aid cuts, and cultural norms around burials and touch add to difficulties in stemming outbreakThe warnings from aid groups and healthcare workers in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have been stark, their calls for coordinated international action impassioned.As the country reels from the return of the Ebola virus, there is growing concern that its fragile healthcare system will struggle to cope with an outbreak that expe

stories.headline: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
stories.summary: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi provinceAt least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years.The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. stories.headline: Senegal's president sacks PM and former ally after months-long feud
stories.summary: Bassirou Diomaye Faye also dissolved the government, creating uncertainty amid a debt crisis in the country.

stories.headline: UK officials suggested single market for goods with Europe
stories.summary: Industry figures briefed on the move said the idea has not been taken forward at this point, after EU scepticism.

stories.headline: US navy chief says $14bn arms sale to Taiwan paused due to Iran war
stories.summary: Hung Cao told a Senate hearing they were "doing a pause" on the sale to make sure they had munitions for the Iran war.

stories.headline: AI used to fake evidence that ended Korean actor's career, say police
stories.summary: Police are seeking an arrest warrant for a YouTuber who allegedly fabricated evidence to defame actor Kim Soo-hyun.

stories.headline: Record 274 climbers scale Everest via Nepal in one day
stories.summary: The summit bonanza comes after a late start to this climbing season due to earlier route obstructions.

stories.headline: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence

stories.headline: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump renews threat of military action
stories.summary: US secretary of state says president would like a negotiated agreement with Havana but likelihood ‘is not high’The US president, Donald Trump, and the secretary of state, Marco Rubio, on Thursday again raised the spectre of military intervention in Cuba, a renewed threat that takes on greater weight a day after the administration announced criminal charges against Raúl Castro, the island’s former leader.“Other presidents have looked at this for 5

stories.headline: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation effortsWhen the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action.“The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuationsHeavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of

stories.headline: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

stories.headline: Sabalenka cuts media short as French Open players protest
stories.summary: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

stories.headline: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.headline: Judge dismisses criminal case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
stories.summary: A federal judge says the Maryland man who was wrongfully deported last year is being prosecuted for political reasons.

stories.headline: Papua New Guinea warns against fishing in New Ireland after mystery deaths of marine life
stories.summary: Initial testing found evidence of metals in water samples, months after province’s residents began reporting unusual numbers of dead fish washing ashorePapua New Guinea’s government has warned communities not to fish from parts of the New Ireland coastline as preliminary tests show evidence of metals in some water samples, after months of residents reporting dead marine life in the area.On 7 May the fisheries minister, Jelta Wong, said initial te

stories.headline: Jailed Vietnamese tycoon's Birkin bags sell for more than $550K
stories.summary: The disgraced tycoon is serving a life sentence for embezzling from a major Vietnamese bank.

stories.headline: Lebanon says 21 killed, including children, in Israeli air strikes
stories.summary: Twelve people were killed in a single strike on a house, including three children and three women, Lebanon's state-run news agency says.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
stories.summary: Russia and China showed they were shoulder-to-shoulder on the world stage, but it became clear there are limits, says the BBC's Russia Editor.

stories.headline: Are Putin and Xi best friends?
stories.summary: Two BBC correspondents break down the China-Russia relationship.

stories.headline: The deadly plane attack at the centre of Castro's indictment
stories.summary: Raúl Castro was armed forces minister when Cuban military jets shot down two civilian planes operated by exiles of the communist country.
